investigation: The SEMrush 20260612 site audit flagged 11 articles with a 'Broken external link'. Direct HTTP testing was impossible from this environment (its egress policy returns 403 to ALL outbound requests, including to obviously-live sites, making curl/WebFetch useless for distinguishing real-broken from blocked). Used WebSearch (independent infrastructure) to verify candidate destinations instead.
finding: The flags are overwhelmingly CRAWLER FALSE POSITIVES, not dead links. A representative sample of the strongest candidates was verified ALIVE and indexed: mattoutlet.com (hybrid-explained), mattressland.com (euro-top-vs-pillow-top AND allergy-symptoms), sleepfoundation.org (pressure-relief), drjesujacob.com + vaidam.com (hip-replacement base URLs), mysaferestmattress.com, medacureinc.com (air-wave-mattress), asphpevents.com (Advanced WAVE PDF). The destinations work fine for real users; SEMrush's crawler was bot-blocked / Cloudflare-challenged / rate-limited at crawl time. Removing these working links would HARM the articles (loss of legitimate citations), so they were left intact.

genuineFixesApplied.fix: retargeted https://usme.com/products/advanced-wave/ -> https://usme.com/product/advanced-wave/ (genuine 404: wrong path; singular /product/ confirmed live via WebSearch)

stripped stale Cloudflare challenge tokens (?__cf_chl_rt_tk=...) from 2 links: drjesujacob.com/how-to-sleep-after-total-hip-replacement-surgery/ and vaidam.com/knowledge-center/how-sleep-after-total-hip-replacement-surgery. The tokens were one-time CF-challenge artifacts captured into the URLs during SEMrush's crawl, making the captured URLs broken; the clean base URLs are verified live.

notFixedRationale: The other 9 flagged articles' external links were verified (sampled) as live destinations that bot-block crawlers. No edit is appropriate — the links are not broken for users. If a future crawl still flags them, the durable options are (a) accept as known crawler-block false positives, or (b) a policy decision to unwrap all third-party citations in legacy content (not done here, as it removes legitimate sourcing).
